channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Deterministic vs Agentic: The Quiet Architectural Bet Every AI Agent Company Is Making
item.description: Every "AI agent" product on the market is making one of two architectural bets, and the founders usually can't articulate which. The bet decides whether your agent costs cents or dollars per run,...

item.title: Show HN: Browser-based light pollution simulator using real photometric data
item.description: Hi HN — author here. iesna.eu is a browser-based ecosystem for working with photometric data: parsing standard luminaire files (LDT/EULUMDAT, IES LM-63, Oxytech, ATLA-S001), running design...

item.title: 47 commits. Zero users. Then 13 days changed the math.
item.description: Three months of commits. Private repo. Never deployed. That's not a story about a bad project. The project was fine — a UAV mission planner that could generate flight paths and export KML files....

item.title: Using PostGIS with Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: With libelo we are building a platform for discovering parks and nature highlights. Almost all entities in our platform have a location attached: parks have boundaries, highlights like waterfalls...

item.title: Linux Firewall Configuration with UFW and iptables
item.description: Protecting your servers from unauthorized access is a fundamental responsibility for any developer or system administrator. This article will guide you through configuring Linux firewalls using...

item.title: How to Backtest Crypto Strategies by Market Regime (With Real Data)
item.description: How to Backtest Crypto Strategies by Market Regime (With Real Data) Your backtest says Sharpe 2.0. You deploy. It immediately starts losing money. Sound familiar? The problem isn't your strategy....

item.title: How to Add Market Regime Filtering to Any Crypto Trading Strategy
item.description: How to Add Market Regime Filtering to Any Crypto Trading Strategy Every backtest looks great until you run it live through a choppy market. The strategy that returned 40% in a trending quarter...

item.title: Pentesting a private tracker: Nuxt.js, Cloudflare and 3 vulnerabilities found
item.description: A private BitTorrent tracker is a web application like any other: authentication, REST API, WebSockets for real-time chat, CDN in front. The difference with a standard SaaS? Members share...

item.title: AI Is Very Good at Implementing Bad Plans
item.description: Most AI coding posts focus on the code: which model writes cleaner functions, which one needs less prompting, which one hallucinates less. But the code isn't usually where my projects break. The...

item.title: Hybrid LLM Routing: Ollama + Claude API Without Quality Degradation
item.description: The bill arrives at the end of the month You ship a bot. Claude responds well, the client is happy. The first month goes by quietly. Then you open Anthropic billing: $200+ for traffic from a small...

item.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
item.description: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. It said it "recently identified" the compromise of its...

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now:...

item.title: Splatoon Raiders preorders for the Switch 2 are nearly 20 percent off
item.description: Nintendo recently announced a new pricing policy, which knocks $10 off the cost of digital versions of future first-party titles exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2. Splatoon Raiders, for instance,...

item.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
item.description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
item.description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had...

item.title: Microsoft tests redesigned Windows 11 Run menu with dark mode and more
item.description: Microsoft is testing its much-needed refresh for the Windows 11 Run menu, offering a modern interface that the company says is faster and comes with support for dark mode. The redesign is rolling...

item.title: Apple raises the Mac Mini’s starting price
item.description: Apple's Mac Mini now starts at $799 after the company pulled the $599 option with 256GB of storage from its online store, as spotted earlier by MacRumors. The model's discontinuation comes just...

item.title: A Dark-Money Campaign Is Paying Influencers to Frame Chinese AI as a Threat
item.description: Build American AI, a nonprofit linked to a super PAC bankrolled by executives at OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz, is funding a campaign to spread pro-AI messaging and stoke fears about China.

item.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents...

item.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
item.description: The outage has hampered communication concerning a critical vulnerability that gives root.

item.title: Birdfy’s smart bird feeder is down to its best-ever price for Mother’s Day
item.description: From crafting machines to smart calendars, a number of unique gifts are already on sale in the run-up to Mother’s Day, May 10th. Birdfy smart feeders are another discounted option to consider,...

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The...

item.title: Amazon’s built-in AI price history expands to show the entire last year
item.description: Amazon's built-in price tracking feature now allows you to see how much a product's price has changed over the past year. To use the feature, open the Amazon app and select the "Price history"...

item.title: Dreame — the vacuum company — just ‘launched’ its own phones
item.description: Dreame, a Chinese manufacturer best known for its robot vacuums but with ambitions to do much more, says it's making smartphones now. I'm not sure I believe it. The company showed off two phones...

item.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in...

item.title: This accessory can snap a Steam Controller to your phone — or almost anything else
item.description: Valve's new Steam Controller goes on sale on Monday for $99, and accessories-maker Mechanism will be ready. As far as we know, Mechanism's new Basegrip is the very first way to attach a Steam...

item.title: Players from the NBA, NFL, and MLB call for a ban on betting ‘unders’
item.description: The unions backing professional NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, and MLS players are calling on the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to ban prediction market platforms from allowing users to bet on...

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

item.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
item.description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore....

item.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
item.description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable...
